reviews.review_msg: HE IS AMAZING BEST PROF EVER!!!!! SUPER AWESOME GUY AND HE DESERVES THE WORLD!!!!!!!

I really enjoyed this class with him. Instructions were clear and quizzes and exams were pretty easy. He always looked for feedback on the class to try and improve it for next time. I highly suggest taking him and also ENGL 397 too!

Really understanding guy, who explains topics well. He's so approachable with questions and I would 10/10 recommend going to his office hours. We had 2 quizzes which you really just had to study the slides to do well on them. The finals also aren't long at all, it's just 3-5 short paragraph questions that you can complete within 40 minutes.

Fantastic prof, super nice guy and really interesting lectures. he facilitated great discussions and made sure everyone felt comfortable sharing. Sometimes class got a bit off topic but not his fault. Really helpful feedback and just overall great guy. Definitely recommend.

Definitely one of my favorite profs. Prof Collis is very dedicated and passionate. Classes are fun and assignments are graded fairly. He builds students up and offers great constructive comments on assignments.

One of the greatest instructors I have ever had. Prof Collis is absolutely fantastic and explains ideas in such clear ways. He encourages student perspectives and listens attentively. He genuinely cares so much about his students and wants everyone to succeed. Fair marker, great feedback. Just a 10/10, wonderful prof.

He's a fun professor to have and is extremely understanding; wants his students to succeed. Lots of reading - this is a class on long poems so we often would have an entire (or half) book to read for class. Lectures were chill and discussion-based.

Bloated, unsubstantial lectures. His class was a huge waste of my time, money and energy! It didn't seem like he put much work into having material to teach, but hey all the poets were his friends so why not just get them on Zoom instead of working? In nearly every lecture there were maybe 5-10 minutes of material. So disappointed and angry!

dr collins is a fantastic lecturer. i had a TA so i am not sure how he grades but he is really understanding, since he moved the deadlines back for several of our assignments. very nice guy, great lectures, cool class overall
Had Eng 112. It was pretty good. The class average was a B. Not hard at all to get the A if you start your assignments ahead of time.

Real talk Collis is a great prof and very kind overall, however the TA'S make or break your grade in this class. The assignments and papers aren't completely easy but also not too difficult as long as you put time and effort into them. Also, don't worry if you aren't a good writer, this class will help you.

Dr. Collis is an amazing prof! I've never considered myself to be strong with writing, going back to high school, yet I managed to get a good grade in this course. He is helpful with responding to our concerns in understanding the poems read in class, and gives fun lectures.

The marking criteria is not clear. Whatever the professor and TA's say to do for the assignments are contradicted in their marking and feedback. They mark very hard, and you are constantly trying to impress them and read their mind. We don't learn anything, and have to figure out the poems by ourselves and whatever we interpret is always wrong.
Dr. Collis is a real gem! I was a bit iffy about which english class/prof to go with, but he is very caring towards all students. Marking is fair and I've gained a real appreciation for poetry.

So disappointed... I saw so many good reviews of Stephen on this site, but it looks like this semester he decided to approach the course differently. The course is so politically heavy that if I dropped in for one lecture, the last thing I would think of it to be is an English course. As a result, tutorial discussions are extremely dry :(

A fantastic professor and a fantastic person too! Although some of my peers weren't very interested in the course itself, I can guarantee that they all had a very positive experience with Steve. His passion and approachability are what make him a great prof and I'd recommend taking a course with him any day!

Stephan is such a knowledgable prof & caring person. He is super clear with his expectation and is very open to discussions. I wouldn't miss class because it's enjoyable and you learn a lot about the world/life through the readings he chooses. Super interesting. He's passionate, approachable, and provides tons of feedback. Visit his office hours.

I have taken one course with Professor Collis and I am upset that it was so late into my university career. He made me love attending class. Very fun man but also has a great knowledge of what he teaches. Makes you want to participate in class. Very thankful to have taken a course with him

Super passionate about the course content which made lectures engaging, and really helped us understand complicated stories/poems. Just 2 essays a final, and a tutorial where you have to participate. Keep up with the readings and listen in lecture and you'll be fine

This guy is amazing. Great lectures, passion, and purpose in the lecture - he's also a great human being. Favourite english course so far. There are books to read, and course is based on them, but they are interesting and well related to the lectures.

Don't take his class if you just want to passively listen to a lecture. If your into writing and want to explore what that means than take his class. He'll give you decent feedback and give you a space to explore your own uniques writing style rather than give negate your creative experience. Yes you might have to think a little!

Stephen Collis is the poet of my dreams. He's hilarious, handsome, kind, and passionate about poetry, especially concerning women writers. His passions really do present themselves in his lectures. You'll find that the readings are actually fun to read and discuss. I never skipped a class!

I loved Stephen! the course i took was 4 hours once a week which was a bit hard to sit through. Stephen himself is amazing though, he is a great lecturer and a very interesting guy. I wasn't crazy about the readings as it was all poetry. He is a pretty fair marker.

He is a fantastic prof with a true passion for the courses that he teaches. Really supportive and values creativity. Was so pleased to have experienced a course with him. I wish I could have taken more.

i do not reccomend this class. poetry from a spatial perspective which was SUPPOSED to be north american literature. he is nice, and he is passionate, but he turned it into a sociology class, i learned nothing, he couldn't link the soc and english texts, AND class lectures were not mandatory or tested on. Easy A, but not worth sitting through.
Nice Guy. Terrible Poet and terrible lecturer. Completely unprepared, spent significant amounts of time showing us YouTube videos and rambling without purpose. Who gave this guy tenure?
A guy who makes five times what you'll ever make dropping poetry for the 99%. Drop the class. Start again. We need a new culture.

This was an ok class, the lectures at first were helpful but after the first essay they became pointless. The thing that bothered me about this class is the prof tries to force his views on the students, it was basically course teaching environmentalism. Not a Gpa booster butif u need an eng credit and dont wont to do a lot of work this is a choice
It's an interesting course, relatively easy. As long as you stay on top of the readings, you should do fine. Final is fair except you won't know which poems he's going to make you analyze with.

great guy, take notes in lecture however because when it comes down to the final, you have to identify random lines out of the 40 poems you have to read and write paragraphs on them. the essays if you start them on the last day, you'll end up with a C+. do them earlier and you will do not bad.
Great Prof!! extremely halpful..classes were fun! 2 essays in the course. If you really work hard on them, you can get an easy A
Dr. Collis was engaging, interesting, funny and insightful. He brought in many wonderful guest poets and his lectures were engagindg. I enjoyed him and his teaching style.
Interesting prof.

Stephen is a great prof! He does go a little overboard with the modern stuff but he's so passionate about it that it's difficult not to get interested. He's a totally nice guy, very smart, funny, laid- back, and HOT! I loved going to class b/c he tried to incorporate diff types of media to teach: poetry, novels, film, etc. Highly recommend!!

Although some of the modern stuff was a bit hard to get a head around, I really liked this guy's style, he tries to push the envelope with some of the experimental poetry, and really digs deep into the meanings and such- I found it very enlightening!
This guy bugged me. Nothing wrong with his teaching, just his attitude. Unless you are a huge fan of modern poetry and Stephen Collis, don't take his class. WW>SC
No Comments
plays favourites ... it is true he'll make time for you anytime. It may help if you're pretty

he is a great guy, will talk to you in the halls, even take you for coffee at the Renaissance stand if you smile nicely. Great course if you want to play along and pay lip service to the brilliance of Steve's poetry and that of his friends. Not so great if you're not into local stuff

I wasn't interested in this class before I took it but Steve is an awesome prof & is obviously passionate about the subject. Uses various resources (film,art,etc) to add to lectures. Great guy who loves teaching & very helpful to students. I dislike poetry but he made it manageable. Highly recommend.
Excellent professor. Obviously has a lot of passion for what he is teaching. At times was even able to make poetry fun...Didn't realize that was possible.
bah, i found this course to be tediously boring and steve was useless. although he is a very nice guy.

Collis's poetry class is awesome, basically beccause the tests are an absolute joke and there's no point whatsoever in going to lecture. When I did go, lectures were pretty interesting, and he has a cool way of paralleling "classic" poetry with more contemporary stuff. Fun guy, loves to talk to students in the hall and take suggestions.
this class is awesome and i love it but dont bother going to lectures.

Although I can't bring myself to rate Collis down due to what a nice guy he is, I warn you: If you are taking his poetry class you will start to wonder why you're paying to go to his lectures. Even the people who pay attention barely take notes. Feel free to skip. You won't miss anything.

I can honestly say that Collis is the worst prof I've ever had. I am very intersted in poetry and took this class for that specific reason, but I havent learned ONE thing. If you are looking for an easy A, take this class, if you actually want to learn something and get value for your money, dont bother.

Stevie is awesome, he is totally engaged in his course.  He is happy to hang out with his students and gather teaching tips from them.  the course is laughable, but worthwile for the mere fact of being in his presence.  he is totally adorable and a great guy.

Does Bob Dylan protest songs w/o mentioning that Dylan isn't really a protest singer.  Ignorant of better songwriters/performers of the period.  Still in a teenage boy 60s fetish phase.  Time to grow up, Stevie.

Steve's a good guy.  The only English classes I haven't been bored out of my mind were with him.
 Definately recommend taking classes with him.
Yes, he is hot and also really funny.  I found myself laughing out loud in lectures constantly.
Great prof: Doesn't believe in final exams so it was open book.  Easy, yet interesting course. An approachable guy with a refreshing teaching style.  Highly recommended.

What can I say? Steve is a darling- the best prof I have had at SFU and the sexiest prof in the English department. Wonderful, insightful lectures and interesting material. The marking is difficult but you feel like you earned your grade.

the stuff about smoking pot as a kid and Dylan is fun and all that... but not that helpful when it comes to tackling big authors and questions. As a rule of thumb, if the writer isn't his friend, he is not that insightful.
He is very hot!!
Did an entire unit on Bob Dylan Protest songs- how cool is that?  Every class was very interesting.  Great reading list, not mainstream stuff.  He has sexy hands too, as strange as that sounds.
No Comments
Has a Toni Morrison fetish
He teachered me enrgish vury good. Do him as teacher or you stoopid

Looks like a prof from the waist up (sweater vests and shirts), a skater boy from the waist down (cargo pants and vans)...likes to tell the story of the first time he got high with his brother and sister, when he was 4.

refreshing, insightful overview of modern, post-modern and contemporary literature. Lectures give you a feeling of where literature is headed in the 21st century. Interested in the literary world? Take this class.
great prof, interesting lectures, and yeah, not much more to say
Cool guy - not an easy grade.
Despite the 8:30 am monday morning lectures, the class was interesting, and a wonderful selection of reading materials.  WOuld definately take another with him
Great prof, but a difficult marker. Very specific in what he looks for in papers.
Steve is a great prof.
excellent prof, makes lectures exciting, reading material was very interesting not the normal dull english books
One of the best professors I've encountered yet.  Definitely take this course if he teaches!
No Comments
really good prof!  I enjoyed the books too.
Great person, great prof, go to his lectures, ace the final exam
fantastic lectures!
great prof - obviously loves what he does - awesome smile!
He was interesting and comprehensive when he talked about the novels.
really cool, great for first years...should pick better books tho.
Great prof, EXCELLENT in tutorials.  Inspiring and stimulating.
Good prof but don't like some of the TA's
Very friendly and handsome. Nice and attractive guy.Good at poems.
good prof, i enjoy his lectures
GREAT teacher! so interesting, and he's a poet!!
Friendly, helpful, lectures a little boring (too much bg info on books/authors)
very good prof to have for first year english
great prof, really good
Great first year teacher and very friendly
